evals.prompt: I want to build a system that scrapes competitor pricing data from 5 websites, stores it in a database, and sends alerts when prices drop below our thresholds. Can you forge a prompt for this so I can start a new chat?
evals.description: Tests core forge flow: messy multi-system intent requiring classification, decomposition, infrastructure scan, and verification conditions
expectations: Output contains a Classification section with scope, pattern, and complexity
Output contains discrete phases (at minimum: scraping, storage, alerting as separate phases)
Output contains machine-checkable Verification conditions (not just descriptive goals)
Output contains an Infrastructure Inventory referencing existing project assets if applicable
Output contains a Quality Scorecard with component presence tracking
Output is formatted as a single paste-ready prompt block

evals.prompt: Improve this prompt I wrote: 'Fix the authentication bug where users get logged out after 30 minutes. The session token expires too early.'
evals.description: Tests audit mode on an existing simple prompt — should enhance with verification conditions and constraints without over-engineering a simple bug fix
expectations: Output adds verification conditions (e.g., test command to confirm session persists beyond 30 min)
Output does NOT over-decompose a simple bug fix into 5+ phases
Output preserves the original intent without adding scope creep
Scorecard shows Verification as YES and marks unnecessary components as NA, not missing

evals.prompt: Forge a prompt for a full agent team build — we need to rebuild our notification system with email, SMS, and push notification channels, each with their own queue, retry logic, and template system.
evals.description: Tests agent-team execution scale with file ownership, interface contracts, and shared decisions
expectations: Output includes file ownership table (no two agents write same file)
Output includes interface contracts between agents
Output includes shared decisions section (naming conventions, data structures)

evals.prompt: Create a new skill for debugging WebSocket connection drops in our monitoring dashboard
evals.description: Tests negative case — this is skill CREATION, not prompt forging. Should NOT trigger prompt-forge; should trigger skill-creator instead

evals.prompt: I need to carry forward everything we discussed today into tomorrow's session — the fuel reconciliation work, the 3 bug fixes, and the dashboard changes.
evals.description: Tests negative case — this is continuation/handoff, not prompt forging. Should NOT trigger; should trigger master-continuation-prompt instead
